parsed_review.summary: CP00-775 (Migration Platform permission-matrix workflow, Risk B, 40 units, RP25.P06.M03.S09-RP25.P06.M04.S18) passes hardened read-only review. Verified via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) Implementation is descriptor-only. registry.js/service.js define the CP775 binding, requirements (deliverable_counts impl15/ui8/claude_review3/security_audit9/test4/hermes_evidence1 = 40, matching baseline; micro_phase rows M03=22 via PERMISSION_MATRIX_ROWS.slice(8,20)+TEST_ROWS+CLOSEOUT_ROWS=12+2+8, M04=18 via slice(0,18)), descriptor/case-set/hermes/claude/handoff factories via shared helpers. Every row is descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, with raw/source/credential/real-client payloads false and all source-system *_runtime_opened false. (2) authority_boundary: claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false; Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt:false/writes_product_state:false/receipt_shape descriptor_only_migration_no_write; Claude packet read_only:true/promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false. (3) validators.js coverage+descriptor validators enforce identity, distributions, per-row descriptor-only/no-runtime/no-payload, no-leak guards (cross-tenant, permission-decision, audit-body, runtime-render, unauthorized-omission), and gate/handoff drift; the validate script asserts emits_runtime_receipt===false and read_only===true for all packs. (4) model.test.js asserts CP775 runtime-closed (claude not final, human approval required, runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-776, section_count 2, *_runtime_opened false, hermes/claude/handoff), CP774 historical, and contract.validation.valid. (5) command-evidence.json records all required validations exit_code 0 (closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack/npm test/validate/spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane/git diff --check) plus node --check and build. (6) No network/db/credential/process.env/child_process calls in source; tokens are negative attestations only; validator writeFile is build-time contract generation, not product state. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer (not final approver) and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. Manifest honestly reports production_ready:false/runtime_ready:false/in_progress; the pending claude-review-result.json (0-byte) is correctly flagged as not a valid review rather than passed off as valid, and the unrelated LDIP invalid-attempt file is out of scope. The pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so the human-approval authority boundary does not block this CP closeout goal. No P0-P3 findings.
